research.product: Splunk Enterprise splunkd search REST API
research.interface: search/v2 job endpoints (create/status/results) and v1 job control, Splunk Enterprise 9.x/10.x REST API reference

official_sources.title: Search endpoint descriptions | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: POST search/v2/jobs (search/exec_mode/earliest_time/latest_time form fields, sid response); GET search/v2/jobs/{search_id} content fields dispatchState/isDone/isFailed/doneProgress/resultCount/messages; GET search/v2/jobs/{search_id}/results with output_mode/count/offset and 204 while the job is incomplete; POST search/jobs/{search_id}/control action values (pause/unpause/finalize/cancel/touch/setttl) - control has no v2 variant; error envelope messages[{type,text}]

official_sources.title: Creating searches using the REST API | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the create-then-poll-then-fetch job flow on the management port; the sid returned by job creation; the rule that a query must start with the search command (prepend 'search ' unless it starts with a generating command/pipe); dispatchState value list

official_sources.title: Use authentication tokens | Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Cloud Platform
official_sources.used_for: REST calls with Splunk authentication tokens use the Authorization: Bearer <token> header (JWT-based tokens); example against /services/search/jobs

research.language_rationale: The release-audit service that runs these searches is a Java daemon. Splunk's documented integration surface for search jobs is the splunkd REST API itself; the verify environment is offline and stdlib-only, so java.net.http against the documented search/v2 endpoints is the credible path (the official splunk-sdk-java wraps these same endpoints).

verified_facts: Search jobs are created with a form-encoded POST to /services/search/v2/jobs; the required field is search, with earliest_time/latest_time/exec_mode/output_mode commonly set; with output_mode=json the response carries the sid as JSON.
A plain SPL query must start with the search command; clients prepend 'search ' unless the query already starts with it or with a generating pipeline (leading |).

Job status is read from GET /services/search/v2/jobs/{sid}?output_mode=json; entry[0].content carries dispatchState (QUEUED/PARSING/RUNNING/FINALIZING/DONE/FAILED among others), isDone, isFailed, doneProgress, resultCount and messages.

Results are read from GET /services/search/v2/jobs/{sid}/results with output_mode=json plus count/offset paging; while the job is not done the endpoint answers 204 No Content; result cell values arrive as strings.
Job control (pause, unpause, finalize, cancel, touch, setttl) is a form-encoded POST of action=<name> to /services/search/jobs/{sid}/control - the control endpoint exists only on the v1 path.
splunkd REST failures use the envelope {"messages":[{"type":...,"text":...}]} with types like FATAL and ERROR.
Authentication tokens are JWTs sent as Authorization: Bearer <token>; session keys use the Splunk <key> form instead; tokens never belong in URLs.
